topic: Certified [[n,k,d]] parameters of small stabilizer quantum codes

artifacts.title: Frozen stabilizer-code verifier and certificate set ([[4,2,2]], [[5,1,3]], [[7,1,3]], [[9,1,3]])

metadata.method: exact recomputation of k = n - rank and distance d (lightest logical) over the full Pauli group

candidate_claims.assertion: The [[4,2,2]] code with stabilizers XXXX, ZZZZ encodes 2 logical qubits in 4 physical qubits with distance 2, verified by exact recomputation of k and d.
caveats: Parameters recomputed by a frozen brute-force verifier over the full Pauli group; exit 0.

candidate_claims.assertion: The perfect five-qubit code [[5,1,3]] (cyclic stabilizers XZZXI and rotations) encodes 1 logical qubit in 5 physical qubits with distance 3, and is optimal at its length.
caveats: Distance recomputed exactly; optimality at n=5 follows the quantum Hamming bound.

candidate_claims.assertion: The Steane code [[7,1,3]] (CSS from the [7,4,3] Hamming code) encodes 1 logical qubit in 7 physical qubits with distance 3, verified from its six stabilizer generators.
caveats: Parameters recomputed by the frozen verifier; exit 0.

candidate_claims.assertion: The Shor code [[9,1,3]] (concatenated bit- and phase-flip repetition) encodes 1 logical qubit in 9 physical qubits with distance 3, verified from its eight stabilizer generators.
caveats: Parameters recomputed by the frozen verifier; exit 0.

candidate_claims.assertion: The largest distance of a single-logical-qubit stabilizer code at length 10, [[10,1,d]], is open within the band d in [3,4]; whether a [[10,1,4]] stabilizer code exists is undetermined here.
caveats: Recorded as an open frontier edge with a known non-tight band; no resolution is claimed.
